title: Kentrochrosia triangularis
taxonomy: Kentrochrosia triangularis is classified within the family Garciniaceae, a family of flowering plants native to tropical Asia. It belongs to the genus Kentrochrosia, which consists of two species. The species Kentrochrosia triangularis is further classified as an evergreen tree, known for its unique triangular-shaped leaves. Kentrochrosia triangularis is native to the tropical regions of Asia, specifically in Sri Lanka and other parts of the Indian subcontinent. The classification of Kentrochrosia triangularis is also recognized within the broader taxonomic group of Rubiaceae, although it is often placed in its own distinct family Garciniaceae.
uses: Kentrochrosia triangularis has been traditionally used in the Pacific Islands for its medicinal properties. The plant is valued for its ability to treat various health issues, including fever and respiratory problems. Its leaves and flowers are used in local remedies to alleviate symptoms of these conditions. The plant's extracts have also been used to treat skin conditions and wounds. Further research is needed to fully understand the potential medicinal uses of Kentrochrosia triangularis.

intro: Kentrochrosia triangularis, commonly known as the Bunga Raya or Red Allamanda, is a flowering plant species belonging to the family Apocynaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in Southeast Asian medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Kentrochrosia triangularis include alkaloids, glycosides, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Kentrochrosia triangularis is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, or used as a tincture, to treat various health conditions.
